findings.title: Import boundaries clean — no back-imports
findings.description: Imports: hashlib, json, re (stdlib), dataclasses (stdlib), datetime (stdlib). From marketmind: gateway.async_client (chat_flash, chat_pro) — downward to gateway layer. pipeline.prediction_extractor (PredictableHypothesis) — sibling import within pipeline/. No imports from glue layer (app.py) or from shadows/. No sibling imports that create cycles.

findings.title: Data flow: L3 → L4 → L6 chain verified
findings.description: reflection_agent (L3) produces StructuredLesson dicts via vars(lesson) → store.save_lesson() → SQLite lessons table. entity_memory (L4) reads from entity_memories table (separate table). expertise_discovery (L6) reads calibration_data table + entity_memory objects. The data flow is: predictions table → reflection → lessons table. entity_memory updates entity_memories table independently. No cross-contamination between lesson data and entity memory blind spots (entity_memory only stores root_cause taxonomy keys, not updated_belief).

findings.title: Module interface contract is well-defined
findings.description: Two public entry points: run_reflection(prediction, original_analysis) -> StructuredLesson | None, and run_batch_reflection(expired_predictions, store) -> list[StructuredLesson]. Single responsibility: post-mortem analysis of verified predictions. No side effects beyond store.save_lesson(). No module-level mutable state.

findings.title: Module dependency DAG position verified
findings.description: reflection_agent depends on: gateway/async_client (downward), pipeline/prediction_extractor (sibling). reflection_agent is depended on by: orchestration layer (via run_batch_reflection). No circular dependency path exists. The learning_store is passed as a parameter (dependency injection), not imported.

findings.title: run_batch_reflection store parameter is untyped
findings.description: The 'store' parameter in run_batch_reflection is annotated with a comment (# LearningStore) but not a type annotation. Python type checkers won't catch misuse. This is cosmetic — the function only calls store.save_lesson() which exists on LearningStore.
findings.remediation: LOW priority. Add TYPE_CHECKING import of LearningStore for type annotation without circular import risk.

notes: Clean import boundaries, well-defined single-responsibility interface, correct position in the Phase I DAG (L3, consumed by orchestration). The store dependency is injected rather than imported, which is the correct pattern.
